programme.title: Exhuma
programme.desc: The process of excavating an ominous grave unleashes long hidden secrets and dreadful consequences buried underneath. 2024. South Korea. Director: Jang Jae-hyun. Starring: Choi Min-sik.

programme.title: Prison 77
programme.desc: Manuel is sentenced to 20 years for stealing a mear 1200 Euros, prompting him to start a movement. 2022. Spain. Director: Alberto Rodriguez. Starring: Miguel Herran, Javier Gutierrez.

programme.title: The Man Who Definitely Didn't Steal Hollywood
programme.desc: The inside story of a waiter turned movie mogul and the scandal that rocked Hollywood. 2024. UK. Director: John Dower.

programme.title: Amour
programme.desc: George promises to keep his wife Anne out of hospital after her stroke. 2012. France, Germany. Director: Michael Haneke. Starring: Jean-Louis Trintignant, Emmanuelle Riva.

programme.title: An American Pastoral
programme.desc: A vitriolic debate is sparked in a rural Pennsylvanian community over the future of their public schools. 2024. France. Director: Auberi Edler.

programme.title: Unmoored
programme.desc: A successful TV presenter's life is upturned when she confronts her husband about his allegations. 2023. Iceland. Director: Caroline Ingvarsson. Starring: Anna Prochniak, Kris Hitchen.

programme.title: Blanquita
programme.desc: Chilean orphan Blanquita becomes the key witness in a high-profile child trafficking scandal. 2022. Chile, Mexico. Director: Fernando Guzzoni. Starring: Laura Lopez, Alejandro Goic.

programme.title: Barbie Uncovered: A Dream House Divided
programme.desc: The doors of the dreamhouse are opened wide revealing how Jack Ryan was written out of Mattel's history books. 2024. UK. Director: Eddie Hutton-Mills.

programme.title: Winter Boy
programme.desc: In his last year of boarding-school, the death of Lucas' father turns his life upside-down. 2022. France. Director: Christophe Honore. Starring: Paul Kircher, Vincent Lacoste, Juliette Binoche.

programme.title: Mozart's Sister
programme.desc: The story of the other Mozart, Maria-Anna, a child prodigy forgotten to time. 2024. Australia. Director: Madeleine Hetherton-Miau.

programme.title: The Problem With People
programme.desc: Two estranged cousins try to make amends for many generations of family dispute. 2024. Ireland. Director: Chris Cottam. Starring: Colm Meaney, Paul Reiser, Lucianne McEvoy.

programme.title: Barbie Uncovered: A Dream House Divided
programme.desc: The doors of the dreamhouse are opened wide revealing how Jack Ryan was written out of Mattel's history books. 2024. UK. Director: Eddie Hutton-Mills.

programme.title: Memoir Of A Snail
programme.desc: A bittersweet memoir of Grace Pudel - hoarder of snails, romance novels, and guinea pigs. 2024. Australia. Director: Adam Elliot. Starring: Jacki Weaver, Sarah Snook, Charlotte Belsey.

programme.title: Brothers
programme.desc: One of the largest manhunts in modern human history kicks off as two brothers escape 1953 communist Czechoslovakia. 2023. Czech Republic. Director: Tomas Masin. Starring: Oskar Hes, Jan Nedbal.

programme.title: Return Home
programme.desc: A newly-divorced man returns to his hometown to see his brother and sister-in-law. 1990. Australia. Director: Ray Argall. Starring: Dennis Coard, Frankie J. Holden.

programme.title: The Good Sister
programme.desc: Rose and her older brother's close relationship is tested when he is accused of sexual assault. 2025. Germany, Spain. Director: Sarah Miro Fischer. Starring: Giamo Alfredi, Laura Balzer.

programme.title: Chronicle Of The Years Of Fire
programme.desc: A man navigates one of the most devastating and painful times in Algerian history. 1975. Algeria. Director: Mohammed Lakhdar-Hamina. Starring: Yorgo Voyagis, Larbi Zekkal.

programme.title: Scala!!!
programme.desc: The inside story of the infamous sex, drugs and rock 'n' roll repertory cinema which inspired a generation during Britain's Thatcher years. 2023. UK. Directors: Ali Catterall, Jane Giles.

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher must break back into prison to avoid punishment after he is unwittly broken out. 1979. USA. Director: Dick Clement. Starring: Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale, Fulton Mackay.

programme.title: Welcome To The Darkness
programme.desc: In 2003, British band The Darkness took the world by storm, then at the height of their fame, they split up. 20 years on, they tell their story. 2023. UK. Director: Simon Emmett.

programme.title: Brothers
programme.desc: One of the largest manhunts in modern human history kicks off as two brothers escape 1953 communist Czechoslovakia. 2023. Czech Republic. Director: Tomas Masin. Starring: Oskar Hes, Jan Nedbal.
